indicators.name: large open space
indicators.rationale: The open space lacks a dedicated area for focused work, making it difficult to set up a workstation.

indicators.name: glass railings
indicators.rationale: Glass railings are not suitable for placing a laptop or other equipment, as they are not stable and could be easily broken.

indicators.name: low seating
indicators.rationale: The low seating is not suitable for prolonged computer work, as it does not provide proper back support and can lead to discomfort.

indicators.name: dark flooring
indicators.rationale: Dark flooring can make it difficult to see the screen in bright light conditions.

indicators.name: large windows
indicators.rationale: Large windows allow natural light to enter, which can be beneficial for some tasks.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (Solo). While there are some positive aspects such as natural light from the large windows, the lack of a dedicated workspace, unstable glass railings, and low seating make it challenging to set up a comfortable and efficient workstation. The open space also makes it difficult to maintain privacy and focus.
